## DNA extraction and quantification

## Extraction kits

Four commercially available DNA extraction kits were selected to evaluate their performance across different biological matrices, including marine host-associated samples:

-   **MasterPure^TM^ Complete DNA and RNA Purification Kit (# MC85200, Epicentre) and Pathogen Lysis Tubes S (50) (# 19091 Qiagen)**

-   **QIAamp® PowerFecal® Pro DNA Kit (# 51804, Qiagen) = PowerFecal kit**

    The first step of the protocol is mechano-chemical cell disruption through bead beating in lysis buffer. The Inhibitor Removal Technology is then used to remove common substances that interfere with downstream PCR applications. DNA from supernatant is captured on MB Spin Column, washed two times and eluted from the silica membrane.

-   **DNeasy® PowerSoil® Pro Kit (# 47014, Qiagen) = PowerSoil kit**

    The protocol starts with mechano-chemical cell disruption through beat beating in lysis solution. In this kit, Inhibitor Removal Technology is also applied, which should help in removal of organic and inorganic materials such as humic acids, cell debris, and proteins that could interfere with downstream PCR applications. After that, the sample is loaded onto MB Spin Column with silica membrane. DNA is then washed two times and eluted from the silica membrane.

-   **PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit (# K182002, Invitrogen) = PureLink kit**

    Multiple protocols are proposed for this kit. In this case we applied the Gram Positive Bacterial Cell Lysate Protocol for all types of samples. In this protocol, the microorganisms are lysed by a combination of heat and enzymatic disruption (lysozyme and proteinase K). After that, the sample is treated with a cleanup buffer to eliminate PCR inhibitors. The sample is then applied to a PureLink™ spin column. DNA is washed one time and then eluted from the silica membrane.

## DNA quantification

DNA concentration and quality were assessed using two complementary approaches:

- **Nanodrop spectrophotometry**, providing estimates of DNA concentration as well as purity ratios (`A260/A280` and `A260/A230`)
- **Qubit dsDNA High Sensitivity Assay (ThermoFisher)**, providing fluorometric quantification of double-stranded DNA

While Nanodrop measurements were available for all samples, Qubit measurements were only available for a subset of samples. In some cases, Qubit values were reported as *out of range*, indicating DNA concentrations below the detection threshold.

---

# Results

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(readr)
library(DT)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)

dna_clean <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "dna_quantification_clean.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

summary_by_kit <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "summary_by_kit.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

summary_by_kit_species_tissue <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "summary_by_kit_species_tissue.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

purity_pass <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "purity_pass_by_kit_species_tissue.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

friedman_results <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "friedman_results_by_matrix.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

wilcox_results <- read_csv(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "tables", "pairwise_paired_wilcox_by_matrix.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
```


## Overview of the dataset

The dataset comprises DNA quantification measurements obtained from two species (Octopus and Turbot), including two tissue types per species. Each biological replicate was processed using all extraction kits, enabling paired comparisons across methods.

```{r}
#| echo: false

DT::datatable(
  dna_clean,
  caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
    style = "caption-side: top; text-align: left;",
    "Table 1. DNA quantification dataset."
  ),
  rownames = FALSE,
  extensions = "Buttons",
  class = "display compact nowrap",
  options = list(
    pageLength = 10,
    scrollX = TRUE,
    autoWidth = TRUE,
    dom = "<'row'<'col-sm-6'B><'col-sm-6'f>>rtip",
    buttons = list(
      list(extend = "copy", text = "Copy"),
      list(extend = "csv", text = "CSV"),
      list(extend = "excel", text = "Excel")
    )
  )
)
```

::: table-note
Note:
Each biological replicate was processed with all extraction kits.
Swabbing corresponds to technical replicates within each biological sample.
:::

## DNA concentration

### Descriptive statistics 

```{r}
#| echo: false

kable(summary_by_kit, caption = "Table 2. DNA concentration and quality summary by extraction kit.") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

DNA yield varied substantially across extraction kits. Mechanical lysis-based methods tended to produce higher variability in DNA concentration, whereas enzymatic approaches yielded more consistent but sometimes lower values.


```{r}
#| echo: false

kable(summary_by_kit_species_tissue, caption = "Table 3. DNA concentration summary by kit, species and tissue.") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

Differences between kits were strongly dependent on the biological matrix, with mucus-rich samples showing greater variability.

### Visualization 

```{r}
#| echo: false

knitr::include_graphics(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "plots", "nanodrop_by_kit_species_tissue.png"))
```


## DNA purity

### A260/A280 ratio

```{r}
#| echo: false

knitr::include_graphics(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "plots", "nanodrop_by_kit_species_tissue.png"))
```

Lower A260/A230 ratios were frequently observed, particularly in mucus-rich samples. This suggests the presence of residual contaminants such as salts or organic compounds, highlighting the importance of efficient inhibitor removal.

### Purity thresholds

```{r}
#| echo: false

kable(purity_pass, caption = "Table 4. Proportion of samples passing purity thresholds.") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

The proportion of samples meeting standard purity thresholds differed between kits, reflecting variation in purification efficiency.


## Qubit measurements

```{r}
#| echo: false

knitr::include_graphics(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "plots", "qubit_status_by_kit_species_tissue.png"))
```

Qubit measurements were not available for all samples, and a significant proportion of samples fell below the detection threshold. This limits the use of Qubit data as a primary metric for comparison.

```{r}
#| echo: false

knitr::include_graphics(here("analysis", "02_dna_quantification", "plots", "qubit_measured_by_kit_species_tissue.png"))
```

Among measurable samples, Qubit-based concentrations broadly followed trends observed with Nanodrop, although differences in sensitivity must be considered


## Statistical comparisons

To account for the paired experimental design, statistical comparisons were performed at the biological replicate level using non-parametric tests.

```{r}
#| echo: false

kable(friedman_results, caption = "Table 5. Friedman test results by matrix.") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

The Friedman test indicated that extraction method significantly influenced DNA yield and/or quality in several matrices.

```{r}
#| echo: false

DT::datatable(
  wilcox_results,
  caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
    style = "caption-side: top; text-align: left;",
    "Table 6. Pairwise Wilcoxon test results."
  ),
  rownames = FALSE,
  extensions = "Buttons",
  class = "display compact nowrap",
  options = list(
    pageLength = 10,
    lengthChange = TRUE,
    scrollX = TRUE,
    dom = "<'row'<'col-sm-6'B><'col-sm-6'f>>rtip",
    buttons = list(
      list(extend = "copy", text = "Copy"),
      list(extend = "csv", text = "CSV"),
      list(extend = "excel", text = "Excel")
    )
  )
)
```

Pairwise comparisons revealed specific differences between kits, although statistical power was limited in some cases due to sample size and missing data.

## Overall, the results demonstrate that:

DNA extraction performance is strongly dependent on both the extraction method and the biological matrix.
Mechanical lysis-based kits tend to increase variability but may improve DNA recovery in complex samples.
Enzymatic extraction provides more consistent results but may be less efficient in challenging matrices.
Inhibitor removal efficiency is a critical factor, particularly for mucus-rich samples such as skin.

Taken together, these findings highlight the importance of selecting an extraction method tailored to the sample type in order to ensure reliable and reproducible microbiome profiling.








Resultados principales
1) MasterPure fue el kit con mejor rendimiento global

Frente al resto de kits, MasterPure destacó de forma consistente en casi todos los indicadores:

Mayor concentración media por Nanodrop: 111 ng/µL
Mayor concentración media por Qubit: 20.2 ng/µL
Mayor proporción de muestras medidas por Qubit: 45%
Mejor proporción de muestras dentro del rango aceptable A260/A280 (1.7–2.0): 60%
Único kit con una fracción apreciable de muestras con A260/A230 ≥ 1.5: 30%

En comparación, el resto quedó bastante por detrás:

PowerFecal: Nanodrop 39.9 ng/µL; Qubit 12.0 ng/µL
PowerSoil: Nanodrop 22.6 ng/µL; Qubit 9.92 ng/µL
PureLink: Nanodrop 5.14 ng/µL; Qubit 0.464 ng/µL

Dicho sin maquillaje: MasterPure se llevó el partido.

2) La pureza A260/A230 fue mala en general

Aquí está uno de los puntos más importantes. Aunque MasterPure fue el mejor kit, la pureza A260/A230 fue en general baja en todos los kits:

MasterPure: 1.09
PowerFecal: 0.166
PowerSoil: 0.205
PureLink: 0.406

Y en cuanto a porcentaje de muestras con A260/A230 ≥ 1.5:

MasterPure: 30%
PowerFecal: 0%
PowerSoil: 0%
PureLink: 0%

Esto sugiere que, incluso con el kit “ganador”, persiste contaminación o arrastre de compuestos absorbentes a 230 nm (sales, fenoles, carbohidratos, EDTA, guanidina u otros compuestos del proceso/matriz). O sea: más ADN, sí; ADN limpísimo, no exactamente.

3) La relación A260/A280 también favoreció a MasterPure, pero con matices

Media A260/A280:

MasterPure: 1.80
PowerFecal: 1.33
PowerSoil: 2.31
PureLink: 1.37

Y proporción de muestras en el rango 1.7–2.0:

MasterPure: 60%
PureLink: 35%
PowerFecal: 15%
PowerSoil: 15%

Interpretación rápida:

MasterPure es el único que da un perfil razonablemente cercano a lo esperado para ADN relativamente limpio.
PowerFecal y PureLink parecen mostrar más problemas de pureza o baja recuperación.
PowerSoil tiene una media A260/A280 de 2.31, que es sospechosamente alta y no necesariamente “mejor”: puede reflejar artefactos por baja concentración, interferencias o ruido de medida, no pureza real.

Ese 2.31 no es una medalla; más bien huele a medida poco fiable o a muestra problemática.

4) El patrón de Qubit refuerza el resultado de Nanodrop

Como Nanodrop puede sobreestimar concentración cuando hay contaminantes, el dato de Qubit es muy útil aquí. Y resulta que Qubit también coloca a MasterPure en primer lugar:

MasterPure: 20.2 ng/µL
PowerFecal: 12.0 ng/µL
PowerSoil: 9.92 ng/µL
PureLink: 0.464 ng/µL

Eso fortalece bastante la conclusión de que MasterPure no solo parece mejor por absorbancia, sino que realmente recupera más ADN cuantificable.

Resultados estadísticos
5) Hubo diferencias globales significativas entre kits en varias matrices

Los test de Friedman detectaron diferencias significativas entre kits para:

A260/A230 ratio
Octopus – Skin
Octopus – Mantle
Turbot – Skin
Turbot – Gut
A260/A280 ratio
Octopus – Mantle
Turbot – Gut
Nanodrop DNA concentration
Octopus – Skin
Octopus – Mantle
Turbot – Skin
Turbot – Gut

Esto indica que el kit de extracción influye significativamente en el rendimiento, y que ese efecto aparece en varias combinaciones especie-tejido.

6) Pero no hubo comparaciones pareadas significativas tras corrección BH

Esto es muy importante interpretarlo bien.

Aunque el Friedman salió significativo en varios casos, ninguna comparación pareada Wilcoxon sobrevivió a la corrección por múltiples comparaciones.

Eso significa:

sí hay evidencia global de que los kits no se comportan igual
pero no puedes afirmar con solidez estadística, tras corrección múltiple, que un par concreto de kits difiera dentro de cada matriz específica

Traducido al castellano de laboratorio:

“Se ve una señal clara de efecto del kit, pero el tamaño muestral es limitado y no da potencia suficiente para señalar con dedo acusador qué pareja exacta de kits es la responsable en cada matriz”.

Muy típico cuando tienes n = 4–5 individuos pareados por matriz. No es raro en absoluto.

Interpretación técnica
Conclusión principal

MasterPure fue el kit con mejor desempeño global para la extracción de ADN en este conjunto de muestras, mostrando:

mayor rendimiento de ADN por Nanodrop
mayor concentración cuantificada por Qubit
mejor comportamiento en A260/A280
mejor proporción de muestras utilizables según criterios de pureza
Pero…

La calidad/pureza del ADN siguió siendo mejorable, sobre todo según A260/A230, incluso con MasterPure. Por tanto:

MasterPure parece el candidato más fuerte
pero el ADN obtenido no puede considerarse impecablemente limpio
y probablemente convenga valorar pasos adicionales de limpieza si el downstream es sensible
Qué diría yo en un informe o resultados

Te dejo una versión bastante publicable:

Across the four extraction kits, MasterPure showed the best overall performance, yielding the highest mean DNA concentration by both Nanodrop (111 ng/µL) and Qubit (20.2 ng/µL), as well as the highest proportion of Qubit-measured samples (45%). MasterPure also provided the most favorable purity metrics, with 60% of samples falling within the A260/A280 range of 1.7–2.0 and 30% exceeding an A260/A230 ratio of 1.5. In contrast, the other kits showed markedly lower DNA yields and poorer purity profiles, particularly for A260/A230, for which no sample reached the 1.5 threshold except those extracted with MasterPure. Overall, these results indicate that MasterPure was the most effective kit for DNA recovery in this study, although the generally low A260/A230 ratios suggest that co-extracted contaminants remained an issue across kits.

Y para la parte estadística:

Friedman tests revealed significant overall differences among extraction kits for Nanodrop DNA concentration and absorbance ratios in several species–tissue combinations, indicating that extraction method influenced DNA yield and purity. However, no pairwise Wilcoxon comparisons remained significant after Benjamini–Hochberg correction, suggesting that although a global kit effect exists, the study had limited power to resolve specific pairwise differences between kits within each matrix.

Lectura biológica/práctica

Si lo que quieres es decidir qué kit priorizar para secuenciación o trabajo futuro:

MasterPure: claramente el mejor candidato
PowerFecal y PowerSoil: rendimiento intermedio, pero pureza floja
PureLink: muy flojo en rendimiento total; pinta a peor opción de las cuatro
Advertencia sensata

No conviene vender esto como “MasterPure produce ADN perfecto”, porque no es verdad. Lo correcto sería decir:

mejor rendimiento relativo
mejor compromiso entre cantidad y pureza
aunque con pureza subóptima, especialmente en A260/A230

Eso queda serio y no vende humo, que en microbioma siempre viene bien porque bastante teatro trae ya el propio ADN.

Si quieres, te lo convierto ahora en un apartado de Results y otro de Discussion en inglés, listos para pegar en el Quarto.
